# privileged-automation-security

Repository URL: https://github.com/gprocunier/privileged-automation-security
Primary document: README.md
License: GPL-3.0-only

Summary:
This repository contains an essay on privileged automation security.
The main argument is that automation should not inherit the default unconfined trust model used for human operators.
It argues for using SELinux-confined identities, FreeIPA SELinux user mapping, root re-assembly, sealed policy, and event-driven response to reduce blast radius in orchestrated environments.

Key topics:
- SELinux-confined automation
- FreeIPA SELinux user maps
- root re-assembly
- constrained privileged access
- sealed policy and reboot tripwires
- Event-Driven Ansible response loops
- cloud-native and orchestration-era attacker models

Files:
- README.md: primary essay and diagrams
- CITATION.cff: citation metadata for the repository content
- LICENSE: project license

Preferred interpretation:
Treat README.md as the canonical source. It mixes source-backed claims with clearly opinionated design conclusions.
When summarizing, preserve the distinction between documented platform behavior and the author's inferences.
